summary: Iteration 118: Tested Ring Hierarchy Fix and Gender-Aware Relationship Inversion. Backend: 14/14 tests PASSED (100%). All resolve_inverse logic tests pass including male/female/unknown gender fallback cases. Frontend: Beneficiaries page loads correctly with Estate Tree visualization. OrbitVisualization getOrbitLevel correctly assigns: Ring 0 (spouse, parents), Ring 1 (children, siblings, grandparents), Ring 2 (grandchildren, extended family, in-laws), Ring 3 (great-grandparents, friend, other).
passed_tests: Backend: Neutral relations (Cousin, Spouse, Friend) return same value regardless of gender
Backend: Comprehensive gendered pairs (Son→Father/Mother, Grandson→Grandfather/Grandmother, etc) work correctly
Frontend: getOrbitLevel assigns Ring 0 to spouse, wife, husband, partner, mother, father, mom, dad, parent
Frontend: getOrbitLevel assigns Ring 1 to son, daughter, child, brother, sister, sibling, grandmother, grandfather
Frontend: getOrbitLevel assigns Ring 2 to grandson, granddaughter, nephew, niece, uncle, aunt, and in-law variants
Frontend: getOrbitLevel assigns Ring 3 to great-grandmother, great-grandfather, great-grand*, friend, other
Backend: Health check returns status=healthy, database=connected
Backend: /api/auth/me returns user profile with valid token
Frontend: Beneficiaries page loads with Estate Tree visualization
Frontend: No console errors blocking functionality

details: great-grandmother/great-grandfather moved from Ring 1 to Ring 3
friend/other moved from Ring 1 to Ring 3

critical_code_review_comments: getOrbitLevel in OrbitVisualization.js correctly checks great-grand FIRST to prevent substring matching issues
resolve_inverse in estates.py correctly handles all gender cases including empty string
Both frontend and backend implementations match the documented requirements
seed_data_creation: None - used existing data for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ring hierarchy and gender-aware inversion changes verified. getOrbitLevel correctly assigns Ring 3 to great-grandparents, friend, other. resolve_inverse correctly uses benefactor gender for gendered inversions with slash fallback for unknown genders. Rate limiting is aggressive (429 after multiple login attempts) - reuse tokens where possible. OrbitVisualization is visible in beneficiary portal (not benefactor portal) when user has family connections as a beneficiary.
notes: This iteration: Ring hierarchy fix + gender-aware relationship inversion
Both features implemented correctly per requirements
Rate limiting triggered during testing (expected behavior, not a bug)
